title: Shipping RAG Systems to Production
excerpt: A practical playbook for moving retrieval-augmented generation from demo to production with observability and guardrails.
author: Anh Th

sections.heading: Why RAG beats fine-tuning for most products
sections.content: Most teams reach for fine-tuning when they need domain knowledge. In practice, RAG delivers faster iteration, clearer attribution, and lower retraining cost for knowledge that changes frequently.

sections.heading: Reference architecture
sections.content: A production RAG stack typically includes ingestion pipelines, chunking strategy, vector storage, retrieval reranking, prompt templates, and evaluation harnesses tied to business metrics.

sections.heading: Guardrails that matter
sections.content: Policy filters, citation requirements, and human escalation paths prevent the most common production failures. Instrument every stage so you can trace bad answers back to source documents.
